commit.message:
feat(application): implement WorkerDispatchService

Add WorkerDispatchService that claims WORKER_DISPATCH jobs from the queue,
resolves spawn context via WorkerDispatchContextPort, and delegates to
WorkerSupervisorService.spawnWorker(). Follows the existing job-processing
pattern (claim → process → complete/fail) but is async and non-self-
rescheduling since dispatch jobs are created on-demand by the scheduler.

New files:
- ports/worker-dispatch.ports.ts — port interfaces and context types
- services/worker-dispatch.service.ts — service factory and implementation
- services/worker-dispatch.service.test.ts — 11 unit tests

commit.message:
fix(session): guard async busy lock with pcall to prevent permanent deadlock (#21)

All three async.void functions (select_tool, select_session, rename_session)
called the inner _() function bare. If ui_select or ui_input (both async.wrap
callbacks) threw a runtime error, state.busy would remain true permanently,
deadlocking the plugin until Neovim restart.

- Replace bare _() with pcall(_) in select_tool, select_session, rename_session
- Ensure state.busy = false always executes on both success and error paths
- Notify the user with [aiwaku] prefixed ERROR message when an unexpected error occurs
- Add missing ---@async annotation to select_session and rename_session

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Auto-refresh JWT token in background when user roles or role permissions change (#356)

* Initial plan

* feat: auto-refresh JWT token when user roles or role permissions change

- UpdateUserRoles now regenerates security stamp and invalidates cache after
  successful role update, causing the user's next request to get a 401 which
  the frontend interceptor handles by calling RefreshToken
- EditRole now finds all users assigned the edited role and invalidates their
  security stamp caches so their tokens are refreshed with updated permissions
- SecurityStampValidationTransform in gateway now skips stamp validation for
  RefreshToken endpoint to avoid infinite 401 loops during token refresh
- UsersService.RefreshToken now correctly fetches the user with roles/permissions
  and generates a fresh token (removes broken RefreshTokenAsync call that
  compared access token against stored refresh token, always failing)
- Adds tests: UpdateUserRoles_RegeneratesSecurityStampAndInvalidatesCache and
  EditRole_RegeneratesSecurityStampForAffectedUsers

Co-authored-by: PSPTorchinim <47593054+PSPTorchinim@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add RoleWithUsersSpec for safe Users navigation include in DeleteRole
- RolesService.GetRoles now returns List<GetRoleDTO> (caches DTOs to avoid circular refs)
- RolesService.AddRole caches DTOs instead of entities in the All-roles cache
- RolesService.DeleteRole uses RoleWithUsersSpec with null-safe Users check
- RolesController.GetRolesV1 ProducesResponseType updated to IEnumerable<GetRoleDTO>

* fix: correctly extract LoginResponseDTO from API response wrapper in refresh token interceptor

The v1UsersRefreshTokenList() endpoint returns LoginResponseDTOResponse
(shape: { success, data: LoginResponseDTO, message, errors }).
The interceptor was casting response.data as LoginResponseDTO, so user/
accessToken/refreshToken all resolved to undefined → null, clearing
localStorage on every successful token refresh.

Fix: access response.data.data (the inner LoginResponseDTO) so the correct
fields are read and localStorage is updated instead of cleared.

fix(session): reopen sidebar in current tab on toggle (#19)

Previously, state.win_id stored a single global window handle with no
awareness of which tabpage it belonged to. win_visible() only checked
nvim_win_is_valid(), so a sidebar open in tab 1 was still treated as
"visible" when the user moved to tab 2. The first toggle from tab 2
therefore closed the tab 1 window and returned early, requiring a
second toggle to actually open the sidebar in tab 2.

Changes:
- window.lua: add win_visible_in_current_tab() that checks both window
  validity and current tabpage membership.
- session.lua: add local close_sidebar_window() helper; switch
  toggle() and open_session() to use win_visible_in_current_tab() so
  the sidebar is treated as hidden when it lives in another tab.
  open_session() closes any existing sidebar window before opening a
  fresh split in the current tab, moving the sidebar in one press.
  new_session() and clear_context() use the new helper for consistent
  unconditional close behaviour.
- send.lua: switch send_to_session() to win_visible_in_current_tab()
  so send_selection() and send_buffer() also auto-open in the current
  tab rather than acting on a sidebar that exists only in another tab.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add explicit @Inject() to EventsModule services for tsx compatibility

tsx (esbuild) does not support emitDecoratorMetadata, so NestJS cannot
resolve constructor parameters by type alone. Services using explicit
@Inject(TOKEN) worked, but EventsModule services relying on implicit
type-based resolution for ModuleRef received undefined, crashing
QueueWorkerEventsService.onModuleInit on startup.

Add @Inject() to all constructor parameters in:
- QueueWorkerEventsService (EventBroadcasterService, ModuleRef)
- DomainEventBroadcasterAdapter (EventBroadcasterService, ModuleRef)
- EventBroadcasterService (EventsGateway)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix movement masking: always check tiles regardless of grid_offset

ObjGridOffset is axis-agnostic — a nonzero offset from vertical movement
was incorrectly allowing horizontal moves into walls. The tile check is
conservative and never produces false positives, so always apply it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always build Room from current RAM tiles to fix stale door corridors

ZeldaGame.room now always calls Room.create() with current_tiles instead
of preferring the cache via Room.get(). Room.create() short-circuits with
torch.equal() when tiles haven't changed, so the common case is cheap.

This fixes stale corridor tiles when doors open (key used or enemies
cleared) — the cached Room previously retained the locked-door tile
layout, causing movement masking to block valid corridor movement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow movement into cave entry tiles for proactive masking

Cave top tile (0xF3) is above OW_FIRST_UNWALKABLE but the NES triggers
a cave transition via CheckPassiveTileObjects (Z_07.asm:2957-2962) when
Link walks into it.  Treat it as walkable for movement masking so the
agent can enter caves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restrict locked-door-with-key override to doorway corridor

The NES CheckDoorway (Z_05.asm:3755) only fires when Link's
perpendicular coordinate matches the doorway: X=$78 for N/S doors,
Y=$8D for E/W doors.  Without this position check, any room with a
locked door would allow movement toward the door from anywhere in the
room, even when blocked by walls/blocks (e.g. debug_1_45.state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty wavefront when exit seed is blocked by obstacles

The wavefront used a single center exit tile offset off-screen as its
BFS seed.  If obstacles blocked expansion from that one point, the
wavefront never reached the rest of the room (e.g. debug_0_68.state).

Two fixes:
- Use ALL exit tiles as off-screen seeds so the BFS can expand from
  every exit point, not just the center.
- Skip can_move checks when expanding from off-screen seeds since they
  are virtual positions with no real NES tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update scenarios

* Fix pixel-alignment bug in can_link_move_from using exact NES hotspots

can_link_move_from was delegating to can_move which used approximate tile-
coordinate offsets (tr+1 for feet). The NES computes hotspot pixel positions
directly: base_Y = ObjY + $0B, then subtracts $40 for the status bar before
dividing by 8 to get the tile row.

The old formula (py - 56) // 8 + 1 disagreed with the NES formula
(py - 53) // 8 for 62% of Y positions (when (py-64) % 8 < 5), checking
one row too low. This caused false positives — allowing movement the NES
would block.

Rewrote can_link_move_from to compute exact NES hotspot positions:
- Left:  col=(px-8)//8,   row=(py-53)//8
- Right: col=(px+16)//8,  row=(py-53)//8
- Up:    col=px//8,        row=(py-61)//8,  checks col and col+1
- Down:  col=px//8,        row=(py-45)//8,  checks col and col+1

can_move (tile-space) left unchanged for wavefront BFS which is internally
consistent with its approximate offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(matter): resolve SBMD endpoints by device type matching (#174)

The original method used to locate the Matter device endpoint to use for
a resource originated from the method used by Zigbee. Zigbee doesn't
have the device types and parts list attributes which are much better
suited to locate endpoints hosting desired functionality. Our drivers
are for specific device types (on/off light, dimmable light, etc). This
maps directly to the device types attribute on Matter endpoints and is
more reliable than just looking for an endpoint with a target cluster as
before.

- Add endpoint map (sbmdEndpointMap) to MatterDevice, built during
AddDevice() by matching device type lists against driver's
matterMeta.deviceTypes
- Add ResolveEndpointMap() and GetEndpointForSbmdIndex() methods
- Update BindResourceReadInfo, BindWriteInfo, BindExecuteInfo, and
BindResourceEventInfo to accept optional sbmdEndpointIndex parameter
- Pre-resolve Matter endpoint IDs at bind time; device-level resources
fall back to GetEndpointForCluster()
- Store resolvedEndpointId on write/execute bindings for use at
invocation time
- Update SpecBasedMatterDeviceDriver to pass endpoint index for
endpoint-level resources and std::nullopt for device-level resources
- Add MatterDeviceEndpointMapTest with 27 tests covering endpoint map
resolution, index lookup, and all four binding methods
- Add device-type-endpoint-resolution spec to openspec/specs
- Archive endpoints-from-device-types change

Refs: BARTON-358

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786) (#1492)

* fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786)

When go-to-definition is used on a type that lives in a different
assembly from the one FCS reports (e.g. System.Boolean forwarded from
netstandard.dll to System.Runtime.dll), ILSpy's CSharpDecompiler
throws:
  'Decompiling types that are not part of the main module is not supported'

This happens because ILSpy's type system resolves type forwarders
transparently — GetTypeDefinition returns the actual type definition
from the target assembly's module, but the decompiler was created for
the source assembly. The mismatch causes the exception.

Fix: after resolving the type definition, check whether its
ParentModule matches the decompiler's main module. If not (a type
forwarder), retrieve the actual assembly path from
IModule.PEFile.FileName and create a fresh CSharpDecompiler for
that assembly. Resolve the type definition again in the new type
system, then decompile as normal.

If the PEFile path is unavailable or the type cannot be resolved in
the redirected assembly, the original decompiler and type definition
are used unchanged — the existing try/catch will handle any remaining

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: skip unreadable files in rename; remove duplicate log in textDocument/implementation (#1491)

* fix: skip unreadable files in rename and log warnings in textDocument/implementation

Fixes two code quality issues in AdaptiveFSharpLspServer.fs:

1. TextDocumentRename: skip files that cannot be read
   When building workspace edits for rename, if a file cannot be opened
   (e.g., SourceLinked virtual paths like /_/src/Server.fs from external
   packages that FCS references but don't exist locally), return None and
   filter it out rather than generating edits for an unreadable file.
   This prevents client errors when the LSP client tries to apply edits
   to files that don't exist. See issue #1011.

2. TextDocumentImplementation: remove duplicate log + log warning on failure
   - Remove the duplicate 'TextDocumentImplementation Request: {params}'
     log that was emitted twice in the handler.
   - Log a warning when getProjectOptions fails instead of silently calling
     failwith, making these failures visible in server logs.

commit.message:
fix(Windows): makechild.mk が子ディレクトリに適用されない問題を修正

[問題1] cygpath -w によるパス形式の不整合 (prepare.mk)
MAKEPART_MK の取得で cygpath -w を使用するとバックスラッシュ形式になり、
patsubst %/makepart.mk,%/makechild.mk のマッチが失敗して makechild.mk が
一切インクルードされなかった。
cygpath -m (D:/... 形式のフォワードスラッシュ) に変更して修正。

この修正により libsrc サブフォルダの makechild.mk に設定された
NO_LINK = 1 が子ディレクトリに正しく伝播されるようになった。

[問題2] NO_LINK=1 のとき clean がライブラリを削除対象に含める問題 (makelibsrc_c_cpp.mk)
NO_LINK=1 のディレクトリはライブラリを生成しないにも関わらず、
clean ターゲットが libapi.dll 等を rm 対象として表示していた (Linux/Windows 共通)。
CLEAN_COMMON/CLEAN_OS の構築を NO_LINK の有無で分岐して修正。

[問題3] DLL リンク時に OBJDIR が未作成で .exp mv が失敗する問題 (makelibsrc_c_cpp.mk)
ソースファイルを持たないライブラリディレクトリでは obj/md が作られず、
.exp ファイルの移動が失敗した。リンクルールの order-only 前提条件に
\ を追加して修正。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
